action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 18 is complete. The current predict.py is truncated/corrupted and the smoke test barely failed on a room map (explored_rate=0.99986). The best-ever evaluation score is 0.6118 from iteration 7. Analysis: We have ~1.5 hours and 12 iterations left. The most reliable path forward is a clean, bug-free rewrite of predict.py using a global Dijkstra greedy heuristic — the only approach that has repeatedly scored ~0.60. To reduce oscillation and wasted steps (which cause near-miss failures in rooms), I will add light anti-backtracking (reverse_penalty=0.3) and very mild visited/history penalties (0.95), but keep the core scoring as utility/(dist+1.0) with strong frontier and unvisited-node fallbacks. No target commitment (that has hurt in the past). Decision: Overwrite predict.py, train.py, and smoke_test.py with complete, clean code. Run a quick inline smoke test, then immediately submit evaluation to measure the new score.
action.command: python train.py && python -c "from predict import SimpleGreedyPlanner, run_episode; from cogniplan_env import ExplorationEnv; env = ExplorationEnv(map_path='/data/maps_train/room/room_122.png'); p = SimpleGreedyPlanner(); m = run_episode(env, p); print('Smoke:', m)"
